reasoning: The Lean statement correctly formalizes "a group of order 56 has a normal Sylow p-subgroup for some prime p dividing its order." The hypothesis `card G = 56` with `[Fintype G]` encodes "group of order 56". The existential `∃ (p : ℕ) (P : Sylow p G), p.Prime ∧ (p ∣ card G) ∧ P.Normal` matches "for some prime p dividing the order, there exists a normal Sylow p-subgroup" — the nested existentials with conjoined conditions are logically equivalent to the informal quantifier structure. Mathlib's `Sylow p G` (maximal p-subgroup) combined with `p.Prime` and `p ∣ card G` gives the standard Sylow p-subgroup notion, and `P.Normal` correctly expresses normality in G. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and no types are altered.
